elements.section-2411-003: $L^p$ coarse Baum--Connes conjecture for coarsely embeddable spaces
elements.sent-2411-073: In this section, we prove the $L^p$ coarse Baum--Connes conjecture for spaces that coarsely embedds into $\ell^q$-spaces for $q\in[1,\infty)$.
elements.sent-2411-074: In particular, in the special case where $p=2$, we show that the usual coarse Baum--Connes conjecture holds for spaces that coarsely embedds into $\ell^q$-space for any $q\in [1, \infty)$.
elements.sent-2411-075: Our proof is inspired by the work from \cite{Yucoarseembed,willett2020higher .}
elements.sent-2411-076: Let $(X,d)$ be a metric space and $\ell^q=\ell^q(\mathbb N)$.
elements.sent-2411-077: We say that $X$ coarsely embeds into $\ell^q$ if there is a map $f\colon X\to \ell^q$ together with functions $\rho_\pm\colon \R_{\geq 0}\to\R_{\geq 0}$ such that $\lim_{x\to\infty}\rho_-(x)=+\infty$ and \begin{equation}\rho_-(d(x,y))\leq \|f(x)-f(y)\|_q\leq \rho_+(d(x,y)).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-078: Throughout this section, $X$ is assumed to be a complete Riemannian manifold with bounded geometry.
elements.sent-2411-079: In this case, we may furthermore require that $f$ is a topological embedding, the functions $\rho_\pm$ are monotonically strictly increasing, and $\rho_\pm(x)=0$ only if $x=0$.

elements.sent-2411-197: In this subsection, we introduce the twisted $L^p$ Roe algebras and the associated $K$-theoretic maps.
elements.sent-2411-198: A general principle for proving the coarse Baum--Cones conjecture is to reduce the proof to a finite dimensional case.
elements.sent-2411-199: For example, in the case of the coarse Baum--Cones conjecture for spaces that coarsely embed into Hilbert space, the proof can be reduced to the case of coarse disjoint unions of finite metric spaces that uniformly coarsely embed into Hilbert space \cite[Section 12.5]{willett2020higher .}
elements.sent-2411-200: In fact, the exact same reduction process from \cite[Section 12.5]{willett2020higher can be applied to the $L^p$ coarse Baum--Cones conjecture.}
elements.sent-2411-201: In particular, to prove the $L^p$ coarse Baum--Cones conjecture for spaces that coarsely embed into $\ell^q$ with $q\in [1, \infty)$, it suffices to prove the $L^p$ coarse Baum--Cones conjecture for coarse disjoint unions of finite metric spaces that uniformly coarsely embed into $\ell^q$ with $q\in [1, \infty)$.

elements.section-2411-004: Coarse Baum--Connes conjecture for coarsely embeddable spaces
elements.sent-2411-399: In this section, we focus on the $C^*$-algebra version of coarse Baum--Connes conjecture for spaces that coarsely embedds into $\ell^q$-spaces for $q\in[1,\infty)$.

elements.sent-2411-403: In this subsection, we recall the definitions of various geometric $C^*$-algebras.

elements.sent-2411-405: Let $X$ be a proper metric space.
elements.sent-2411-406: An infinite dimensional separable Hilbert space $H$ is called a geometric $X$-module if $H$ is equipped with a non-degenerate representation of $C_0(X)$, and no non-zero element of $C_0(X)$ acts as a compact operator.
elements.sent-2411-407: Let $T$ be a bounded operator acting on $H$.

elements.sent-2411-409: The propagation of $T$, denoted by $\prop(T)$, is defined by the supremum of $d(x,y)$ for any pair $(x,y)\in \supp(T)$.
elements.sent-2411-410: The collection of all locally compact operators with finite propagation over $H$ is denoted by $ C^*_{alg (X)$.}
elements.sent-2411-411: The completion of $ C^*_{alg (X)$ with respect to the operator norm acting on $H$ is called the Roe algebra $ C^*(X)$.}

elements.section-2411-005: $\ell^p$-coarse Baum--Connes conjecture for coarsely embeddable spaces
elements.sent-2411-620: In this section, we prove Theorem \ref{thm:lp-lqcBCIntro for general $p\geq 1$.}
elements.sent-2411-621: In this subsection, we introduce $\ell^p$-Roe algebras and prove the corresponding $\ell^p$-coarse Baum--Connes conjecture.
elements.sent-2411-622: We point out that the $\ell^p$-Roe algebras in the current paper are slightly different from the version of $\ell^p$-Roe algebras introduced by Zhang and Zhou in \cite{Zhangzhoulp .}
elements.sent-2411-623: See the discussion following Conjecture \ref{conj:l^pCBC for more details.}
elements.sent-2411-624: For simplicity, let $P$ be a finite dimensional simplicial complex with bounded geometry, e.g., the Rips complex of a locally finite metric space.
elements.sent-2411-625: Let $Z$ be a countable dense subset of $P$.
elements.sent-2411-626: Denote by $\N$ the set of natural numbers and $H=\ell^2(\N)$.
elements.sent-2411-627: We denote by $\ell^p(Z,\ell^p(\N,H))=\ell^p(Z\times \N,H)$ the completion of simple functions $\varphi\colon Z\times\N\to H$ with the following norm \begin{equation}\|\varphi\|_p\coloneqq \left(\sum_{(z,n)\in Z\times\N}\|\varphi(z,n)\|^p_H\right)^{1/p}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-628: Equivalently, the Banach space $\ell^p(Z,\ell^p(\N,H))$ can be viewed as the space of functions on $Z\times\N\times\N$ with respect to the following mixed $\ell^p$-$\ell^2$ norm: \begin{equation}\|\varphi\|_p= \left(\sum_{(z,n)\in Z\times\N}\left(\sum_{m\in\N}|\varphi(z,n,m)|^2\right)^{p/2}\right)^{1/p}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-629: Each compactly supported bounded Borel function on $P$ acts on $\ell^p(Z,\ell^p(\N,H))$ by multiplication on the first factor $Z$.
elements.sent-2411-630: Analogous to the $\ell^2$ case, we shall use the space $\ell^p(Z,\ell^p(\N,H))$ as the geometric module to construct the corresponding $\ell^p$-Roe algebra.
elements.sent-2411-631: Let $T$ be a bounded operator on $\ell^p(Z,\ell^p(\N,H))$.
elements.sent-2411-632: The support of $T$, denoted by $\supp(T)$, is defined to be the collection of points $(x,y)$ in $Z\times Z$, such that $\chi_VT\chi_U\ne 0$ for all open neighborhoods $U$ of $x$ and $V$ of $y$, where $\chi_U$ and $\chi_V$ are the characteristic functions of $U$ and $V$, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-633: The propagation of $T$, denoted by $\prop(T)$, is defined to be the supremum of $d(x,y)$ for all $(x,y)\in\supp(T)$.
elements.sent-2411-634: Let $P$ be a finite dimensional simplicial complex.
elements.sent-2411-635: Let $ C^p_{alg (P)$ be the collection of all bounded operators on $\ell^p(Z,\ell^p(\N,H))$ that are locally compact and have finite propagation.}
elements.sent-2411-636: The $\ell^p$-Roe algebra of $P$, denoted by $C^p(P)$, is defined to be the Banach algebra obtained by the completion of $ C^p_{alg (P)$ with respect to the operator norm.}
elements.sent-2411-637: The $K$-theory of the $\ell^p$-Roe algebras is invariant under coarse equivalences.
elements.sent-2411-638: This follows from the same proof of \cite[Lemma 2.8]{Zhangzhoulp .}
elements.sent-2411-640: Let $P$ and $P'$ be simplicial complexes and $f\colon P\to P'$ a continuous coarse map.
elements.sent-2411-641: Then $f$ cannonically induces a map \begin{equation}f_*\colon K_*(C^p(P))\to K_*(C^p(P')).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-642: In particular, $C^p(P)$ is independent of the choice of the countable dense subset $Z$.
elements.sent-2411-643: Now we formulate the $\ell^p$-coarse Baum--Connes conjecture.
elements.sent-2411-644: Let $C_L^p(P)$ be the completion of all uniformly continuous paths \begin{equation}T\colon [0,\infty)\to C^p_{alg}(P)\end{equation} such that $\|T(t)\|$ is uniformly bounded and $\prop(T(t))\to 0$ as $t\to\infty$, with respect to the sup-norm \begin{equation}\|T\|\coloneqq \sup_{t\geq 0}\|T(t)\|.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-645: Recall that in Section \ref{sec:preliminary we have introduced the Rips complex $P_r(X)$ at scale $r$ for a locally finite metric space $X$ with bounded geometry.}
elements.sent-2411-646: Note that $P_d(X)$ is coarsely equivalent to $X$, hence the $K$-groups of their $\ell^p$-Roe algebras are canonically isomorphic.
elements.sent-2411-647: We note that the $\ell^p$-Roe algebra defined here differs slightly from the one introduced by Zhang and Zhou in \cite{Zhangzhoulp .}
elements.sent-2411-648: More specifically, the $\ell^p$-Roe algebra of $P$ in \cite[Definition 2.6]{Zhangzhoulp , which we denote by $\mathbf{C ^p(P)$, is defined to be the Banach algebra generated by locally compact operators with finite propagation acting on $\ell^p(Z,\ell^p(\N))$, where $Z$ is a countable dense subset of $P$ as above.}}
elements.sent-2411-649: Similarly, there is a corresponding version of $\ell^p$-localization algebra from \cite{Zhangzhoulp , which we denote by $\mathbf{C _L^p(P)$.}}

elements.sent-2411-652: More precisely, we state the question as follows.
elements.sent-2411-653: Let $C^p(X)$ and $\mathbf C^p(X)$ be the completion of locally compact and finite propagation operators acting on the $C_0(X)$-modules $\ell^p(Z,\ell^p(\mathbb N))$ and $\ell^p(Z,\ell^p(\mathbb N,H))$, respectively.
